intro: Rhadamanthus urgineoides, also known as the prickly poppy, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its spiny stems and leaves. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including anxiety, insomnia, and pain, due to its sedative and anti-inflammatory properties. Rhadamanthus urgineoides contains bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Rhadamanthus urgineoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
